purpose.summary: This batch COBOL IMS program, CBPAUP0C, deletes expired pending authorization messages from the IMS database. It reads pending authorization summary and detail segments, checks if the authorization has expired based on the expiry days parameter, and deletes the detail and summary segments if they qualify for deletion. The program takes checkpoint periodically and at the end of processing, and displays summary statistics of records read and deleted.
purpose.business_context: This program is part of the CardDemo application's authorization module and is used to purge expired authorization records, likely to maintain database performance and reduce storage costs.

inputs.name: SYSIN
inputs.description: Contains parameters for expiry days, checkpoint frequency, checkpoint display frequency, and debug flag.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: IMS segment containing summary information for pending authorizations.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: IMS segment containing detail information for pending authorizations.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
outputs.description: IMS segment containing summary information for pending authorizations (deleted).

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: IMS segment containing detail information for pending authorizations (deleted).

outputs.name: SYSOUT
outputs.description: Displays program start message, parameter values, and summary statistics of records read and deleted.

business_rules.description: An authorization detail record is considered expired and qualified for deletion if the difference between the current date and the authorization date exceeds the expiry days specified in the input parameters.
business_rules.logic_summary: Calculates the difference between CURRENT-YYDDD and PA-AUTH-DATE and compares it to P-EXPIRY-DAYS. Sets QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE flag if the difference is greater.

business_rules.description: If the approved and declined authorization counts in the summary record are both zero, the summary record is deleted.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks if PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT are both less than or equal to 0.

transforms.transformation_description: Calculates the difference between the current Julian date and the authorization date.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the layout of the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY segment.
Defines the layout of the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the main control paragraph of the program. It orchestrates the process of deleting expired pending authorization messages. It first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to perform initial setup tasks like accepting the current date and parameters from SYSIN. Then, it enters a loop that reads pending authorization summary records using 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY. Inside this loop, it reads pending authorization detail records using 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL and checks if each detail record is expired using 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED. If a detail record is expired (QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE), it is deleted using 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL. After processing all detail records for a summary record, it checks if both the approved and declined authorization counts in the summary record are zero. If so, it deletes the summary record using 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY. Periodically, based on the checkpoint frequency parameter (P-CHKP-FREQ), it takes a checkpoint using 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT. Finally, after processing all summary records, it takes a final checkpoint and displays summary statistics.

This paragraph performs the initialization tasks for the program. It accepts the current date from the system using the ACCEPT statement and stores it in CURRENT-DATE and CURRENT-YYDDD. It also accepts the program parameters from SYSIN, storing them in the PRM-INFO group item. These parameters include the expiry days for pending authorizations (P-EXPIRY-DAYS), the checkpoint frequency (P-CHKP-FREQ), the checkpoint display frequency (P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ), and a debug flag (P-DEBUG-FLAG). Finally, it displays a starting message to the console, including the parameters received. No error handling is performed in this paragraph. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

error_handling.condition: IMS return code indicates an error

open_questions.question: What are the exact layouts of the CIPAUSMY and CIPAUDTY copybooks?
open_questions.context: The program uses these copybooks to define the IMS segment structures, but the copybooks themselves are not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: The copybook definitions need to be provided to fully understand the program's data structures.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the IO-PCB-MASK and PGM-PCB-MASK linkage section variables?
open_questions.context: These are passed to the procedure division but not used in the visible code.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the IMS PSB definition to understand how these PCBs are used.

open_questions.question: What is the logic within paragraphs 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY, 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL, 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL, 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY and 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT?
open_questions.context: The code only shows that these paragraphs are performed, but not what they do.
open_questions.suggestion: Need to see the source code for these paragraphs to understand their functionality.

dead_code.name: 9999-EXIT
dead_code.reason: Paragraph '9999-EXIT' is never PERFORMed or referenced by any other paragraph or program

dead_code.name: IO-PCB-MASK
dead_code.reason: Record layout 'IO-PCB-MASK' is never used by any program

dead_code.name: PGM-PCB-MASK
dead_code.reason: Record layout 'PGM-PCB-MASK' is never used by any program

dead_code.name: PRM-INFO
dead_code.reason: Record layout 'PRM-INFO' is never used by any program

call_semantics.purpose: Initializes program variables by reading current date, parameters from SYSIN, and setting default values for expiry days, checkpoint frequencies, and debug flag.
Displays a debug message with the count of summary records read if debug mode is enabled.
Retrieves the next pending authorization detail record via IMS GNP call, updates read counter and control flags based on retrieval status.
Checks if the current authorization detail is expired based on date difference and updates counts accordingly, marking it for deletion if expired.
Deletes the current authorization detail segment via IMS DLET call and increments the deletion counter if successful, otherwise abends.
Reads the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database and updates counters and flags accordingly.
Deletes the current pending authorization summary record from the IMS database and increments the deletion counter if successful.
Takes an IMS checkpoint and displays success or failure based on the current authorization processing count and application ID.
Displays a debug message with the current count of authorization summaries read if debug mode is enabled.
Takes an IMS checkpoint and displays success or failure based on the current authorization processing count and application ID.
Abends the program with return code 16 when called.
Abends the program with return code 16 upon failure to read an authorization detail segment.
Abends the program with return code 16 if deletion of an authorization detail segment fails.
Abends the program with return code 16 if deletion of an authorization summary segment fails.
Abends the program with return code 16 if the checkpoint operation fails.
